summary: The team is sprinting toward a September launch of the joint 'Going Global' report with IE Business School and the Asia Start campaign, while redesigning the knowledge website and prioritizing SEO for key programs.
summaryDetail: The biggest initiative is the joint report with IE Business School on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia and Tyson events in Singapore. The team is translating the report and preparing op-eds and case studies. Meanwhile, the Asia Start campaign is launching with two parallel routes (AI and traditional), and the new Intelligent China program website is being built. Urgent this week: finalizing feedback on Joker's knowledge website redesign (due to similarity issues), selecting a social media agency (Media Minds preferred), and processing the UNICON membership invoice. Upcoming: finalizing the Global Program Brochure for Dean Xiang's feedback by July 14, and evaluating social media ad agency proposals by July 15.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: The team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, finding it too similar to the current site. They discussed incorporating elements from Joseph's more editorial-style design to show more articles and indices.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing programs in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Team discussed a joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'. They plan to include a main research piece, op-eds from deans and president, and industry case studies, aiming for a September launch at events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Campaign Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with modules in Beijing, Shanghai/Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market cooperation.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep the two-menu structure (Knowledge menu and School menu).
Show more articles on the homepage, with a pinned article and latest articles below.
Bring indices (BCI, BSI) to the knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Include Berkeley program as a third priority if resources allow.
Shun Tian Shu press release will incorporate the 10 points into the current release, not create a separate article.
Media Minds is the preferred social media agency due to budget constraints and time sensitivity.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
